name: GenRecon
readme_summary: GenRecon is a Python-based framework for high-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view RGB images. It leverages strong generative 3D priors, specifically Trellis.2, by conditioning the generation process on localized scene chunks to achieve scene-scale reconstruction. The approach enables faithful, editable PBR mesh reconstructions of indoor environments, outperforming existing methods.
key_features: High-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view images
Integration of strong generative 3D priors (e.g., Trellis.2)
Scene reconstruction via spatially-localized, overlapping chunks
Projection-based conditioning mechanism for 3D feature lifting
Generation of faithful, editable PBR mesh reconstructions
primary_use_case: To reconstruct detailed and editable 3D meshes of indoor scenes from multiple 2D images by combining generative modeling with scene decomposition.
cover_image_prompt: A master sculptor in a digital workshop, meticulously carving a complex 3D indoor scene from raw, multi-colored light beams emanating from various camera lenses. The sculptor uses a holographic interface, projecting a grid of glowing, interconnected cubes that represent the scene's chunks. As the sculptor refines the form, the light beams coalesce into a detailed, PBR textured mesh, with subtle code snippets and data visualizations floating around the workshop. The overall mood is one of creation and precision, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with soft, focused lighting.

name: torlink
description: A sleek, zero-setup torrent finder and downloader that lives right in your terminal.
readme_summary: torlink is a terminal-based torrent finder and downloader that simplifies the process of locating and downloading torrent files. It searches a curated list of reputable sources, presents results clearly, and handles downloads directly within the terminal, offering a zero-setup, zero-configuration user experience.
key_features: Curated list of reputable torrent sources
Direct magnet link and infohash support
primary_use_case: To provide a fast, simple, and private way to find and download torrents directly from the command line without any complex setup.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot character sitting comfortably in a cozy armchair within a dimly lit, modern terminal environment. The robot is holding a glowing, transparent tablet displaying a curated list of digital icons representing movies, games, and music. From the tablet, streams of data particles flow directly into a sleek, minimalist download icon on the robot's lap, which shows a progress bar. Subtle lines of code and binary patterns are visible on the terminal screen behind the robot, and the repository name 'torlink' is subtly integrated into the terminal's interface. The overall mood is one of effortless discovery and seamless downloading. The art style should be a clean, 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear visual metaphors and a calming color palette.

name: L0p4Map
description: Professional network monitoring & visualization tool. L0P4Map combines high-speed ARP discovery with full nmap integration and a real-time interactive network topology engine. Works on both local networks and custom IPs/websites.
readme_summary: L0p4Map is a professional network monitoring and visualization tool designed for security researchers. It combines high-speed ARP scanning, hostname resolution, device fingerprinting, and role detection with a comprehensive nmap integration for deep network analysis and vulnerability assessment.
primary_use_case: Network monitoring, visualization, and security analysis for security researchers.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant cartographer in a dimly lit, high-tech observatory, meticulously drawing a detailed, glowing map of an unseen city. Instead of traditional landmarks, the map displays interconnected nodes representing devices, with glowing lines showing network traffic flow and subtle red alerts highlighting vulnerabilities. Small screens around the observatory show real-time data feeds and nmap scan results. The cartographer uses a special magnifying glass that reveals hidden network structures. The scene is rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a dark, professional color palette, emphasizing clarity and depth.

name: codebase-memory-mcp
description: High-performance code intelligence MCP server. Indexes codebases into a persistent knowledge graph — average repo in milliseconds. 158 languages, sub-ms queries, 99% fewer tokens. Single static binary, zero dependencies.
readme_summary: Codebase-Memory-MCP is a high-performance C-based code intelligence engine that indexes entire codebases into a knowledge graph for AI coding agents. It achieves extreme indexing speeds and sub-millisecond query times, supporting 158 languages with enhanced semantic resolution for popular ones, and ships as a single, zero-dependency static binary.
key_features: High-performance code indexing (milliseconds for average repo, minutes for Linux kernel)
Support for 158 languages via tree-sitter AST analysis
Hybrid LSP semantic type resolution for enhanced accuracy
primary_use_case: To provide AI coding agents with fast, efficient, and comprehensive code intelligence by indexing codebases into a navigable knowledge graph.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing an infinitely vast library where each book represents a file in a codebase. The librarian uses glowing threads of light to connect related books, forming intricate, glowing constellations that represent functions, classes, and dependencies. In the background, a small, futuristic AI assistant consults these constellations, instantly retrieving information. The scene is bathed in a soft, ethereal glow, with subtle digital particles flowing around the librarian's hands. The library shelves are made of sleek, modern materials, hinting at advanced technology. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clarity and wonder, emphasizing the transformation of raw code into structured, accessible knowledge.

name: rlm
description: General plug-and-play inference library for Recursive Language Models (RLMs), supporting various sandboxes.
readme_summary: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) offer a novel inference paradigm for language models, enabling them to handle near-infinite context lengths by programmatically examining, decomposing, and recursively calling themselves. This library provides an extensible inference engine and training environment for RLMs, supporting various sandboxes and standard API-based or local LLMs.
key_features: Task-agnostic inference paradigm for language models
Programmatically examines, decomposes, and recursively calls itself
Offloads context as a variable in a REPL environment
Supports interaction with and launching of sub-LM calls
Compatibility with standard API-based and local LLMs
primary_use_case: To provide a general plug-and-play inference library for Recursive Language Models (RLMs), enabling language models to process and interact with extremely long contexts by recursively calling themselves within a sandboxed environment.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient librarian meticulously organizing an infinite library where books represent data and shelves stretch into the horizon. The librarian doesn't just read books; they open them, revealing smaller, self-contained libraries within, symbolizing recursive calls. Glowing threads of information connect these nested libraries, illustrating the programmatic examination and decomposition of context. Subtle digital particles and faint code snippets float around, hinting at the underlying AI and computational nature. The scene is bathed in a warm, intellectual light, with a clear focus on the librarian and the immediate, infinitely branching structure of knowledge. The style is a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing depth and complexity in a clean, organized manner.

name: universal-android-debloater-next-generation
description: Cross-platform GUI written in Rust using ADB to debloat non-rooted Android devices. Improve your privacy, the security and battery life of your device.
readme_summary: Universal Android Debloater Next Generation (UAD-ng) is a cross-platform GUI application written in Rust that uses ADB to remove pre-installed system apps (bloatware) from non-rooted Android devices. It aims to improve device privacy, security, and performance by reducing the attack surface and freeing up resources.
key_features: Improves privacy, security, and battery life
Reduces attack surface by removing system apps
primary_use_case: Removing unwanted system applications (bloatware) from Android devices to enhance privacy, security, and performance, without requiring root access.
cover_image_prompt: A digital gardener carefully pruning overgrown, tangled vines (representing bloatware) from a healthy, vibrant Android device tree. The gardener uses glowing pruning shears connected to a laptop screen displaying a clean, organized list of apps. Small, digital particles representing improved performance and privacy float upwards from the pruned branches. The background is a clean, minimalist workspace with subtle code snippets visible on a secondary monitor. The overall mood is one of restoration and optimization. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style with a bright, clean color palette and clear, defined elements.
